title: Algebra 2 Unit 4 Lesson 1 Quiz
lesson_title: Inverse Variation and the Reciprocal Function
description: Five-question Lesson 1 quiz aligned to the verified Unit 4 Lesson 1 content that appears on the Topic 4 assessment.

questions.prompt: The chart represents an inverse variation. Find the value of p. x-values: 3, 10, 15, 30. y-values: 5, p, 1, 0.5.
Write an equation for the inverse variation represented by the table. x-values: -3, -1, 1/2, 2/3. y-values: 4, 12, -24, -18.
Three students can wash a car in 16 minutes. If the time varies inversely with the number of students washing the car, how many minutes will it take two students to complete that same job?
What is the equation of the horizontal asymptote of the graph y = 1/(x+5) - 4?
questions.answer_key_note: For y = a/(x-h) + k, the horizontal asymptote is y = k.

questions.prompt: The graph of y = 1/x is translated 3 units up and 2 units to the left. What is an equation of the translated graph?
